statement.p: A biologist has been observing a trees height. This type of tree typically grows by 0.11 feet each month. Eleven months into the observation, the tree was 20.01 feet tall.

Let x be the number of months passed since the observations started, and let y be the trees height at that time, in feet. Use a linear equation to model the trees height as the number of months pass.
This lines slope-intercept equation is .
25 months after the observations started, the tree would be feet in height.
months after the observation started, the tree would be 24.96 feet tall.

li.p: A lines equation in slope-intercept form looks like y=mx+b, where m is the slope, and y is the y-coordinate of the y-intercept. Since the tree grows at a rate of 0.11 feet per month, 0.11 is the slope of the linear model.
So now we have that y=0.11x+b. The next step is to find the value of b. There are at least two ways.
One way is to substitute a known point into y=0.11x+b. We use the point (11,20.01).

p.m: \begin{aligned} y \amp = 0.11x + b \\ 20.01 \amp = 0.11 \cdot 11 + b \\ 20.01 \amp = 1.21 + b \\ 20.01\mathbf{{}-1.21} \amp = 1.21 + b\mathbf{{}-1.21} \\ 18.8 \amp = b\\ b \amp = 18.8 \end{aligned}

li.p: This lines slope-intercept equation is y=0.11x+18.8.
Another way is to use the lines point-slope equation y-y_{1}=m(x-x_{1}). Again, we use the point (11,20.01).
This is the second way to find b, and thus the lines slope-intercept equation is y=0.11x+18.8.
After 25 months, we can find the height of the tree if we substitute 25 in for x in the equation y=0.11x+18.8. We have:
So after 25 months, the tree is feet tall.
To find when the tree was feet tall, we can substitute 24.96 in for y in the equation y=0.11x+18.8, and we have:
So the tree was 24.96 feet tall after 56 months.
